subject: Project Management
title: Pull vs Push
description: A quick, practical guide to Pull vs Push.

hook.text: In a push system, managers assign work. In a pull system, workers take work when ready.

buildup.text: Push overloads people. Work piles up, wait times grow, and quality drops under pressure.

discovery.text: Pull systems let teams take the next item only when they have capacity. Flow replaces force.

twist.text: Pull feels slower at first. But total delivery time drops because nothing waits in a queue.

climax.text: Pull is counterintuitive: doing less at once gets more done overall.

quiz.question: Why do pull systems often outperform push systems?
options: Items don't sit in queues — total delivery time drops
Workers are forced to work faster
